title: Authenticating with the Atlas Go SDK

sections.heading: Using the Atlas Go SDK in Your Code with Digest Authentication
(Preview) Using the Atlas Go SDK with Service Account Authentication
sections.body:
Atlas SDK Go provides OAuth Authentication using Service Accounts. This
is currently available as a [Preview](https://www.mongodb.com/resources/beta-programs) feature. To learn
more, see [Preview Features](preview-features).

A Service Account implements an OAuth [client\_credentials](https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/client-credentials) grant.
For more information about the feature, please refer to [Service Account Public documentation.](atlas/api/api-authentication)

sections.heading: Authenticating with OAuth ClientID and ClientSecret
Admin API Authentication using Service Accounts
Revocation
OAuth Token Cache
sections.body:
Service Account OAuth Access Tokens validity is expressed as a number of seconds in the  `expires_in` field.
Clients can cache these Access Tokens to mitigate rate limiting and adhere to [Access Token limits](manual/reference/limits).

For an example on how to cache and reuse OAuth tokens in the Atlas SDK for Go, see the Service Account OAuth Token Cache
[Service Account OAuth Token Cache example](https://github.com/mongodb/atlas-sdk-go/tree/main/examples/service_account_token_store).
